LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT PROVED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH IT'S CLAIM OF BAD FAITH CLAIMS HANDLING. EVEN IF THERE WAS A GENUINE DISPUTE, SUCH THAT THE DENIAL OF THE CLAIM WAS NOT IN BAD FAITH, DEFENDANT'S MOTION DOES NOT DISPROVE THE ALLEGATIONS THAT ITS DELAY IN PROCESSING AND PROVIDING INFORMATION TO PLAINTIFF WAS IN BAD FAITH. Off Calendar Apr-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
John Hook, Esq., appeared for defendant/opposing party: Colony Insurance Co.
Alexander Berline, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and moving party: Vikas, LLC

RULING:
HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Colony Insurance Company's Motion For Protective Order To Prevent Deposition Of Irene K. Yesowitch With Specified Documents is granted. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR.

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Its Answer To The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. ALEXANDER J. BERLINE OF HANSON, BRIDGETT, TEL. NO. 995-5035 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN HOOK OF LONG & LEVIT, TEL. NO. 397-2222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Leave To Amend Its Answer To The First Amended Complaint IS GRANTED, ON CONDITION THAT DEFENDANT AGREE TO COOPERATE IN PROVIDING REASONABLE DISCOVERY TO PLAINTIFF ON THE ADDED DEFENSES NOTWITHSTANDING THE DISCOVERY CUT OFF AND SHOULD PLAINTIFF CHOOSE TO REQUEST IT, THAT DEFENDANT NOT OPPOSE A REASONABLE TRIAL CONTINUANCE OR DEFENDANT AGREES TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT LESS THAN 75 DAYS NOT LATER THAN MAY 21, 2008. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING WORKING OUT A SCHEDULE AND IF THEY CANNOT AGREE, THEY COULD COME TO THE EX-PARTE CALENDAR. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =(301/PJB/PM)

Off Calendar Mar-18-2008 Text Ruling

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT PROVED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH IT'S CLAIM OF BAD FAITH CLAIMS HANDLING. EVEN IF THERE WAS A GENUINE DISPUTE, SUCH THAT THE DENIAL OF THE CLAIM WAS NOT IN BAD FAITH, DEFENDANT'S MOTION DOES NOT DISPROVE THE ALLEGATIONS THAT ITS DELAY IN PROCESSING AND PROVIDING INFORMATION TO PLAINTIFF WAS IN BAD FAITH.
